content.full_text:
.

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

September 15, 2004

Jeffrey R. Dodge
PACE International Union
Post Office Box 349
Martinez, CA 94553-0034

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-04-185

Dear Mr. Dodge:

This letter is in response to your request on behalf of PACE International Union for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

You are the political director for the PACE International Union's local political action committee and have asked whether the method used by the organization to raise funds for its local political action committee complies with the law.

DISCUSSION

Your letter states that, as approved by a vote of your membership, $0.50 per member per month of your regular monthly union dues is deposited in a segregated fund for the purposes of funding your state and local political activities. Expenditures from this fund are subject to the recommendation of your executive board and the approval of your membership at a regular union meeting.

Labor unions and other organizations that receive contributions totaling $1,000 or more in a calendar year qualify as "committees" under the Act and must file periodic reports disclosing their financial activities. (Section 82013.) The term "contribution" includes any portion of dues paid by members of an organization that are earmarked or

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-04-185
Page No. 2

used for the purpose of making contributions and expenditures to support or oppose state
and local candidates and ballot measures. (Section 82015; regulation 18215.)

The Act does not specify or prohibit any particular fund-raising method. As long
as all receipts and expenditures are properly disclosed and the organization complies with
the Act's recordkeeping requirements and the applicable contribution limits, the
fundraising method you describe would not violate the provisions of the Act. (Sections
84101, 85303.)

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

Enclosure
